channel.title: Javascript on xow
channel.description: Recent content in Javascript on xow

item.title: Undefined and control flow in Javascript (why undefined when let x = 3) ?
item.description: Question When you type let x = 3 (declare a variable) in a console of Chrome/Firefox/ often you get a mysterious undefined. It’s the same case when you type function f(){} (function declaration). The response can be quite intimidating as this answer here. Here is a simple explanation. Completion type and control flow In Javascript, the Completion type is a Record used to explain the runtime propagation of values and control flow such as the behaviour of statements (break, continue, return and throw, normal) that perform nonlocal transfers of control.
